steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage says Gogol carves his name into clay and leaves his signature in paint — both describe making a visible mark.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Both "carves" (etches a name into clay) and "leaves" (puts a signature in paint) mean making a visible indication. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — ‘Marks’: makes a visible sign or impression — both carving into clay and leaving a painted signature are forms of marking. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Passes’: moves by or goes beyond — Gogol is not moving past objects; he is adding something to them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Turns’: rotates or transforms — neither carving nor leaving a signature involves rotating anything. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Follows’: goes after or imitates — Gogol is creating original signatures, not following something. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
